title: Kickoff & Intro to Competitive Programming
summary: We discussed the goals of the team, introduced everyone, and covered the main platforms we will be using (USACO, Codeforces). Learned about competitive programming formats and the standard input/output models.

title: Time Complexity & Big O Notation
summary: A deep dive into measuring algorithm efficiency. Covered time and space complexity, how to calculate Big O, and practical examples of O(1), O(N), O(N log N), and O(N^2) algorithms to avoid TLE (Time Limit Exceeded) errors.

title: Arrays, Vectors, and Basic Data Structures
summary: We explored static vs dynamic arrays in C++ (std::vector) and Java (ArrayList). Discussed how dynamic sizing happens under the hood and best practices for memory allocation during contests.

title: Introduction to Sorting & Custom Comparators
summary: Learned about standard sorting library functions (std::sort, Collections.sort) and how to write custom comparator functions. Used sorting to optimize brute-force search problems.

title: Binary Search & Two Pointers
summary: Reviewed linear search, then proved binary search efficiency. Wrote binary search algorithm from scratch to avoid off-by-one errors. Also covered the two pointers approach for sliding window optimization.

title: Graph Theory Basics
summary: Modeled real-world connections using graphs. Covered vertices, edges, directed vs undirected graphs, and how to represent them efficiently using Adjacency Lists in code.

title: BFS & DFS Traversal Techniques
summary: Implemented Breadth-First Search (using a Queue) and Depth-First Search (using Recursion/Stack). We practiced traversing grids, finding connected components, and tracking visited states.

title: Greedy Algorithms and Optimization
summary: Analyzed problems where making the locally optimal choice leads to the globally optimal solution. Learned patterns and common pitfalls where greedy approaches fail and when to prove a greedy choice.

title: Intro to Dynamic Programming & Prefix Sums
summary: Transitioned from overlapping subproblems to optimal substructure. Practiced calculating 1D and 2D prefix sum arrays for fast range queries, and basic memoization techniques.

title: Mock USACO Bronze & Strategy Session
summary: Simulated a real 4-hour contest environment using past USACO Bronze problems. Reviewed debugging techniques, reading problem statements carefully, and dealing with edge cases.
